---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---


```{r loading packages}

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(survival)
  library(tibble)
  library(readr)  
  library(tidyverse)  
  library(cmdstanr)
})
```

```{r loading data}
rm(list = ls())
source("methods/dtest.R")

set.seed(42)
bundle_path <- "data/reconstructed_ipd/MD_RSV_LRTI.rds" 

bundle <- readRDS(bundle_path)

stopifnot(is.list(bundle))

stopifnot("ipd" %in% names(bundle))

ipd_df <- bundle$ipd

str(ipd_df)
head(ipd_df)
```


```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 02. BASIC QC (BEFORE DURHAM)
# ======================================================================

stopifnot(all(c("time", "status", "treat") %in% names(ipd_df)))

ipd_df |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    events = sum(status == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    time_min = min(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    time_max = max(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_missing = sum(is.na(time) | is.na(status) | is.na(treat))
  ) |>
  print()


ipd_df |>
  count(treat, status) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = status, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>
  print()


unique(ipd_df$treat)
```


```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 03. PREP DATA (Durham toolkit)
# ======================================================================

dat <- durham_prep_data(
  data = ipd_df,
  time_col = "time",
  status_col = "status",
  treat_col = "treat",
  ref_level = "placebo",
  vax_level = "nirsevimab"
)

levels(dat$treat)

```

```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 04. OVERALL COX MODEL (Durham toolkit)
# ======================================================================
cox_sum <- durham_fit_cox(dat)

cat("\n==================== OVERALL COX SUMMARY ====================\n")
cat("N:      ", cox_sum$n, "\n")
cat("Events: ", cox_sum$events, "\n")
cat("HR:     ", signif(cox_sum$hr, 5), "\n")
cat("VE(%):  ", round(cox_sum$ve, 2), "\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")


summary(cox_sum$fit)
```

```{r}

# ======================================================================
# 05. PH / WANING TEST (Grambsch–Therneau via cox.zph)
# ======================================================================
ph <- durham_ph_test(cox_sum$fit, transform = "identity")

cat("\n==================== PH TEST (cox.zph) =======================\n")
cat("p_treat:  ", signif(ph$p_treat, 4), "\n")
cat("p_global: ", signif(ph$p_global, 4), "\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")

print(ph$zph$table)

plot(ph$zph, resid = FALSE)
abline(h = 0, lty = 2)

```

```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 06. DURHAM CURVE (time-varying beta(t), HR(t), VE(t))
# ======================================================================

main <- durham_curve(
  dat,
  smooth = "loess",
  span = 0.75,
  zph_transform = "identity"
)

# The curve you’ll use for plotting/inspection:
curve_main <- main$curve
head(curve_main)

# Optional: inspect the residual process used to construct beta(t)
head(main$residual_process)

```

```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 07. BOOTSTRAP BANDS (pointwise 95% CI)
# ======================================================================
boot <- durham_bootstrap_bands(
  dat,
  t_grid = curve_main$time,
  B = 500,
  smooth = "loess",
  span = 0.75,
  zph_transform = "identity",
  seed = 42
)

cat("\n==================== BOOTSTRAP ===============================\n")
cat("B requested: ", 500, "\n")
cat("Successful replicates (non-empty): ", boot$n_success, "\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")

# Merge bootstrap bands with the main curve
curve_ci <- curve_main |>
  mutate(
    VE_lower = boot$ve_lower,
    VE_upper = boot$ve_upper
  )

# Quick look at uncertainty width
curve_ci |>
  summarise(
    mean_width = mean(VE_upper - VE_lower, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_width  = max(VE_upper - VE_lower, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  print()

```

```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 07. Log log plot
# ======================================================================


km_fit <- survfit(
  Surv(time, status) ~ treat,
  data = ipd_df
)

km_df <- data.frame(
  time  = km_fit$time,
  surv  = km_fit$surv,
  treat = rep(names(km_fit$strata), km_fit$strata)
)

km_df$treat <- sub("treat=", "", km_df$treat)

# Avoid log(0)
km_df <- km_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(surv > 0)

ggplot(km_df, aes(
  x = log(time),
  y = log(-log(surv)),
  colour = treat
)) +
  geom_step(direction = "hv", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "log(Time)",
    y = "log(-log(Survival))",
    colour = "Treatment",
    title = "Log(-log) survival plot for PH assessment"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

```

```{r}

# ======================================================================
# 08. ONE-SHOT RUN 
# ======================================================================
res <- durham_waning_analysis(
  data = ipd_df,
  time_col = "time",
  status_col = "status",
  treat_col = "treat",
  ref_level = "placebo",
  vax_level = "nirsevimab",
  smooth = "loess",
  span = 0.75,
  zph_transform = "identity",
  B = 500,
  seed = 42
)

# Print key results
cat("\n==================== DURHAM ONE-SHOT =========================\n")
cat("HR:     ", signif(res$cox$hr, 5), "\n")
cat("VE(%):  ", round(res$cox$ve, 2), "\n")
cat("Events: ", res$cox$events, "\n")
cat("PH p_treat:  ", signif(res$ph$p_treat, 4), "\n")
cat("PH p_global: ", signif(res$ph$p_global, 4), "\n")
cat("Bootstrap success: ", res$bootstrap$n_success, "\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")

summary(res$curve$VE_t)


```

```{r}
# ======================================================================
# 09. PLOTS (Durham VE(t) with CI bands)
# ======================================================================

print(plot_durham_ve(res))
#print(plot_durham_ve(res, ylim = c(50, 100)))
```



```{r}
dat <- durham_prep_data(ipd_df)

main_spl <- durham_curve_spline(dat, spline="ns", df=3)
boot_spl <- durham_bootstrap_bands_spline(dat, t_grid=main_spl$curve$time, B=500, spline="ns", df=3)

curve_ci <- main_spl$curve %>%
  mutate(VE_lower = boot_spl$ve_lower,
         VE_upper = boot_spl$ve_upper)

```

```{r}
dat <- durham_prep_data(ipd_df)
dte <- durham_bootstrap_bands_moutofn(dat) 

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Main Durham curve on the same grid as the bootstrap
main <- durham_curve(
  dat,
  t_grid = dte$t_grid,      # ensure same grid
  smooth = "loess",
  span = 0.75,
  zph_transform = "identity"
)

# 2) Merge main curve + m-out-of-n CI bands
curve_ci <- main$curve %>%
  mutate(
    VE_lower = dte$ve_lower,
    VE_upper = dte$ve_upper
  )

# (optional) clamp for nicer plotting (prevents wild negatives)
curve_ci <- curve_ci %>%
  mutate(
    VE_lower = pmax(VE_lower, -100),
    VE_upper = pmin(VE_upper,  100),
    VE_t     = pmin(pmax(VE_t, -100), 100)
  )

# 3) Plot
ggplot(curve_ci, aes(x = time, y = VE_t)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = VE_lower, ymax = VE_upper), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    title = "Durham VE(t) with m-out-of-n bootstrap 95% bands",
    subtitle = paste0("B=", dte$B, ", m_frac=", dte$m_frac),
    x = "Days since dose",
    y = "Vaccine efficacy (%)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

```

```{r}
# main curve already computed above: main$curve$VE_t

sim <- bootstrap_simultaneous_bands(
  main_vec = main$curve$VE_t,
  boot_mat = dte$boot_mat,
  level = 0.95
)

curve_ci2 <- main$curve %>%
  mutate(
    VE_lower_sim = sim$lower,
    VE_upper_sim = sim$upper
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    VE_lower_sim = pmax(VE_lower_sim, -100),
    VE_upper_sim = pmin(VE_upper_sim,  100),
    VE_t         = pmin(pmax(VE_t, -100), 100)
  )

ggplot(curve_ci2, aes(x = time, y = VE_t)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = VE_lower_sim, ymax = VE_upper_sim), alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(
    title = "Durham VE(t) with simultaneous 95% band (m-out-of-n bootstrap)",
    subtitle = paste0("Band uses max|deviation|; B=", sim$n_success, ", m_frac=", dte$m_frac),
    x = "Days since dose",
    y = "Vaccine efficacy (%)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# TDC (Time-Dependent Coefficient) MODEL
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(survival)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(readr)  
})

set.seed(42)
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

source("methods/tdc.R")

bundle_path <- "data/reconstructed_ipd/MD_RSV_LRTI.rds"   

bundle <- readRDS(bundle_path)
stopifnot(is.list(bundle), "ipd" %in% names(bundle))

ipd_df <- bundle$ipd

# Quick structure checks
stopifnot(all(c("time", "status", "treat") %in% names(ipd_df)))
str(ipd_df)
head(ipd_df)

# ======================================================================
# 02. BASIC QC (before modeling)
# ======================================================================
qc <- ipd_df |> 
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    events = sum(status == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    time_min = min(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    time_max = max(time, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_missing = sum(is.na(time) | is.na(status) | is.na(treat))
  )
print(qc)

# Events by arm
ipd_df %>%
  count(treat, status) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = status, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  print()

# Confirm treatment labels present
print(unique(ipd_df$treat))

# ======================================================================
# 03. TDC: MAIN ONE-SHOT ANALYSIS (recommended workflow)
# ======================================================================
# Key knobs:
# - eps: avoids log(0). Use small value (e.g., 1e-3 days) or (better) 0.5 days.
# - B: bootstrap replicates
# - seed: reproducibility
ref_level <- "placebo"
vax_level <- "nirsevimab"

B    <- 500
seed <- 42
eps  <- 1e-3

res_tdc <- tdc_logt_analysis(
  data       = ipd_df,
  time_col   = "time",
  status_col = "status",
  treat_col  = "treat",
  ref_level  = ref_level,
  vax_level  = vax_level,
  B          = B,
  seed       = seed,
  eps        = eps
)

# ======================================================================
# 04. PRINT MODEL OUTPUT (b0, b1) + INTERPRETATION HELPERS
# ======================================================================
fit_obj <- res_tdc$fit_obj

cat("\n==================== TDC MODEL (log time interaction) ====================\n")
cat("Endpoint file: ", bundle_path, "\n")
cat("N:            ", nrow(fit_obj$data), "\n")
cat("Events:       ", sum(fit_obj$data$status), "\n")
cat("eps:          ", fit_obj$eps, "\n\n")

cat("b0 (main vax effect):     ", signif(fit_obj$coef["b0"], 6), "\n")
cat("b1 (vax * log(time)):     ", signif(fit_obj$coef["b1"], 6), "\n")
cat("Interpretation:\n")
cat("  log HR(t) = b0 + b1*log(t)\n")
cat("  If b1 > 0: HR(t) increases with time => VE wanes\n")
cat("  If b1 < 0: HR(t) decreases with time => VE increases\n")
cat("==========================================================================\n\n")

# Full Cox summary (includes p-values for b0 and tt(vax))
summary(fit_obj$fit)

# ======================================================================
# 05. TDC PREDICTIONS AT SPECIFIC TIMES (report-friendly)
# ======================================================================
# Pick clinically meaningful time points (days)
times_report <- c(7, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150)

pred_report <- tdc_predict_logt(fit_obj, t_grid = times_report)

cat("\n==================== VE(t) at selected times ============================\n")
print(pred_report)
cat("==========================================================================\n\n")

# Optional: add to a neat table
pred_report %>%
  mutate(
    HR_t = round(HR_t, 3),
    VE_t = round(VE_t, 1)
  ) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

# ======================================================================
# 06. BOOTSTRAP DIAGNOSTICS
# ======================================================================
boot <- res_tdc$boot
cat("\n==================== BOOTSTRAP ===============================\n")
cat("B requested: ", B, "\n")
cat("Replicates with any output (current counter): ", boot$n_success, "\n")
cat("NOTE: This counter is lenient (counts partial outputs). For strict success,\n")
cat("      use: sum(colSums(is.na(boot$boot_mat)) == 0)\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")

n_success_full <- sum(colSums(is.na(boot$boot_mat)) == 0)
cat("Strict full-grid successes: ", n_success_full, " / ", B, "\n\n")

# ======================================================================
# 07. PLOT VE(t) WITH BOOTSTRAP BANDS
# ======================================================================
print(plot_tdc_logt(res_tdc, ylim = c(0, 100)))
print(plot_tdc_logt(res_tdc, ylim = c(50, 100)))

# Optional: save plots
# ggsave("tdc_ve_full.png", plot = plot_tdc_logt(res_tdc, ylim = c(0, 100)),
#        width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
# ggsave("tdc_ve_zoom.png", plot = plot_tdc_logt(res_tdc, ylim = c(50, 100)),
#        width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# ======================================================================
# 08. COMPARE TDC vs CONSTANT HR COX (sanity check)
# ======================================================================
# Constant HR model:
fit_const <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ treat, data = fit_obj$data, ties = "efron")
hr_const  <- unname(exp(coef(fit_const))[1])
ve_const  <- (1 - hr_const) * 100

cat("\n==================== SANITY CHECK ============================\n")
cat("Constant Cox HR: ", signif(hr_const, 5), " (VE=", round(ve_const, 1), "%)\n")
cat("TDC model b0,b1:  ", round(fit_obj$coef["b0"], 4), ", ", round(fit_obj$coef["b1"], 4), "\n")
cat("If b1 is near 0, TDC reduces to constant HR.\n")
cat("=============================================================\n\n")

# Likelihood ratio comparison: constant vs time-dependent coefficient model
# (Note: not nested in the strictest sense for partial likelihood with tt(),
#  but this gives a useful indication; you can also compare AIC.)
cat("AIC constant: ", AIC(fit_const), "\n")
cat("AIC TDC:      ", AIC(fit_obj$fit), "\n\n")

# ======================================================================
# 09. EXPORT RESULTS (curve + model)
# ======================================================================
# Save analysis object
out_rds <- "data/tdc_results_MD_RSV_LRTI.rds"  # <-- rename per endpoint
saveRDS(res_tdc, out_rds)

# Save curve as CSV for reporting
out_csv <- "data/tdc_curve_MD_RSV_LRTI.csv"    # <-- rename per endpoint
readr::write_csv(res_tdc$curve, out_csv)

cat("Saved:\n")
cat(" - ", out_rds, "\n")
cat(" - ", out_csv, "\n")

# ======================================================================
# 10. OPTIONAL: BATCH RUN MULTIPLE ENDPOINTS
# ======================================================================
endpoint_files <- c(
  "data/reconstructed_ipd/MD_RSV_LRTI.rds",
  "data/reconstructed_ipd/RSV_Hospitalization.rds"
  # Add others if you have them
)

batch <- lapply(endpoint_files, function(fp) {
  b <- readRDS(fp)
  ipd <- b$ipd

  r <- tdc_logt_analysis(
    data = ipd,
    time_col = "time", status_col = "status", treat_col = "treat",
    ref_level = ref_level, vax_level = vax_level,
    B = B, seed = seed, eps = eps
  )

  # summary row
  b0 <- r$fit_obj$coef["b0"]
  b1 <- r$fit_obj$coef["b1"]

  # VE at 150 days for comparability
  ve150 <- tdc_predict_logt(r$fit_obj, t_grid = 150)$VE_t

  tibble(
    endpoint = if ("endpoint" %in% names(b)) b$endpoint else basename(fp),
    n = nrow(r$fit_obj$data),
    events = sum(r$fit_obj$data$status),
    b0 = as.numeric(b0),
    b1 = as.numeric(b1),
    ve_150 = as.numeric(ve150),
    aic = AIC(r$fit_obj$fit)
  )
})

batch_summary <- bind_rows(batch)
print(batch_summary)

# Optional export
# readr::write_csv(batch_summary, "data/tdc_batch_summary.csv")

################################################################################
# END
################################################################################

```

# --------------------------------
# Logistic
# -------------------------------

```{r}
source("methods/logistic.R")

res_logit <- logit_tdc_analysis(
  data = ipd_df,
  time_col = "time", 
  status_col = "status",
  treat_col = "treat"

   )
```



```{r}
library(rstan)
rstan::rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
source("methods/bayesian.R")

pp_obj <- pp_make(ipd_df, dt = 7)

fit_stan <- fit_bayes_stan(
  pp_obj,
  df_baseline = 8,
  df_waning = 6,
  iter = 4000, warmup = 1500,
  chains = 4, seed = 42
)


```

```{r}
print(fit_stan$fit, pars = c("alpha0","beta0","sigma_a","sigma_d"))
```

```{r}
curve_stan <- extract_ve_curve_stan(fit_stan)
print(plot_ve_stan(curve_stan))
```
